description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, metallic fuselage with a slightly angular nose and a long, narrow tail boom. The main rotor consists of four slender, evenly spaced blades extending horizontally from the top of the body, while a tail rotor is mounted at the end of the tail boom. The landing skids are robust and tubular, running parallel beneath the fuselage and connected by crossbars for stability. The cockpit is located at the front, with dark, angular windows that wrap around the nose, providing visibility for the pilot and co-pilot. The exterior is primarily a weathered gray-blue metal, giving it a utilitarian, industrial appearance. No visible registration numbers or markings are present on the body.
description: A metallic, gray-blue helicopter with four main rotor blades, a long tail boom, and robust landing skids. The cockpit features dark, angular windows and the overall design is utilitarian and industrial.
description_view_0: Front view showing the angular cockpit windows, the symmetrical arrangement of the four main rotor blades, and the landing skids beneath the fuselage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated tail boom, the side profile of the cockpit, and the parallel landing skids with visible crossbars.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, the main rotor blades extending outward, and the landing skids from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the tail boom, cockpit side, and the structure of the landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades made of metallic material, colored gray with no visible markings, extending symmetrically from the rotor hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: A small tail rotor is present at the end of the tail boom, with two blades oriented vertically; details are minimal but the blades appear to be metallic and gray.
attribute_landing_skids: Two parallel, tubular landing skids made of metal, colored gray, connected by crossbars for added stability and support.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Three angular cockpit windows wrap around the front of the helicopter, dark-tinted with visible framing, providing wide visibility for the crew.
